title: Shared-score multivalue decode cluster for the Llama7B attention frontier
hypothesis: A shared-score multivalue cluster rooted at `attention_decode_score_multivalue_cluster_int8_m1x8_iterdiv` can preserve the existing signed-int8 Q/K/V, s32 score, LUT-softmax decode contract while eliminating the prior cluster's 16 repeated score fills by storing each M1x8 score row once per 8-token block and replaying it across all 16 value-slice groups.

direct_comparison.primary_question: How much of the local-cluster frontier loss comes from repeating score fill and store 16 times instead of filling once and replaying 16 eight-dimensional value slices from shared score state?
include: one M1x8 score-row fill per 8-token block
shared score storage written once and replayed across 16 value-slice groups
eight-dimensional streamed result beats for each value slice
one iterative divider shared across the replayed value path
unchanged signed-int8 Q/K/V inputs, signed-32 score accumulation, and LUT softmax semantics
repo-portable JSON and Markdown equivalence evidence before any physical sweep
exclude: do not erase or overwrite the committed local-cluster frontier evidence; revise it as a superseded architecture bound only
do not promote vectorless OpenROAD power as token energy
chip-level NoC, clock/reset, and HBM/DRAM composition remain outside this local-cluster step

required_evaluations.objective: Prove one score fill, one score-store write, 16 value-slice replays, streamed eight-dimension outputs, and one iterative divider preserve the integer Llama7B decode contract.

expected_result.reason: The new architecture changes score fill and value replay topology, so equivalence must pass before any PPA spending.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure Nangate45 timing, area, and vectorless power for the shared-score multivalue cluster once equivalence passes.

expected_result.reason: Vectorless power is needed for structural comparison, but token energy remains blocked until activity-backed evidence exists.

required_evaluations.objective: Re-run the shared-score multivalue cluster at 8 ns for the 2.5 mm die / 2.4 mm square core bridge evidence.

expected_result.reason: Collect the missing 8 ns Nangate45 evidence in 2.5 mm envelope while keeping current vectorless metrics as bounded frontier evidence.

required_evaluations.objective: Measure the exact-state activity calibration path with the unique binary-FSM 8 ns sweep, matched to the v2 physical architecture and configuration.

expected_result.reason: The v2 result remains the valid one-hot PPA baseline and is not invalidated. V3 is a matched physical architecture point with SYNTH_ARGS=-nofsm so routed FSM bits correspond to RTL state activity. PR #1413 / merge f281d6f7 enables complete block_weight capture, compact diagnostics, and FSM provenance; the forthcoming v3 sweep implementation is not yet merged.

remaining_abstractions_after_this_step: Score multiplier and shift derivation remain external inputs to the cluster boundary.
Vectorless power is a structural proxy only; token energy remains blocked until switching activity is measured.
The shared-score multivalue cluster still stops at one local block boundary and does not close chip-level transport or memory.
